tldr: Anthropic releases Claude Mythos Preview with cybersecurity capabilities to only 50 vetted organizations under Project Glasswing, citing safety concerns about autonomous vulnerability discovery.
OpenAI launches $100/month ChatGPT Pro tier targeting heavy Codex users while acknowledging Microsoft partnership 'limited' enterprise reach in internal memo.
Meta releases Muse Spark from new Superintelligence Labs, marking shift from open-source Llama to proprietary multimodal assistant for physical world interaction.
Google integrates NotebookLM into Gemini with persistent memory for projects, while launching Gemini 2.5 Flash as cost-optimized reasoning model for high-volume APIs.
Microsoft removes Copilot branding from Windows 11 while launching Claude-powered Copilot Cowork multi-agent framework and testing autonomous task completion features.
AWS expands Bedrock with Claude Mythos preview and Agent Registry to address AI agent sprawl, plus cross-region inference and model distillation capabilities.
Apple AI chief John Giannandrea departs after 8 years as company develops AI smart glasses and iPhone 18 Pro with major camera upgrades.
Mistral AI unifies flagship models into Mistral Small 4 under Apache 2.0 license, supporting text/image inputs with configurable reasoning effort.
NVIDIA leverages own NeMo AI for next-gen GPU design while SiFive reaches $3.65B valuation with open RISC-V AI chip architecture.
Geoffrey Hinton warns of 10-20% chance AI wipes out humanity while community debates benchmark credibility and open-source model competition from China.

news_items.headline: Anthropic Releases Claude Mythos Preview with Advanced Cybersecurity Capabilities Under Restricted Access
news_items.summary: Anthropic unveiled Claude Mythos Preview, its most powerful frontier model demonstrating significant improvements in reasoning, coding, and cybersecurity capabilities. The model can autonomously discover vulnerabilities and develop working exploits across operating systems, browsers, and software. Access is restricted to approximately 50 vetted organizations including Microsoft, Apple, Google, and the Linux Foundation under Project Glasswing. The initiative provides tools and frameworks for vulnerability scanning, threat detection, and secure AI deployment practices, reportedly cutting breach risk by up to 70% and securing over 200 critical systems for major clients.

news_items.headline: OpenAI Launches $100 ChatGPT Pro Tier While Acknowledging Microsoft Partnership Limitations
news_items.summary: OpenAI launched a new $100/month ChatGPT Pro plan targeting heavy Codex users, positioned between the existing $20 Plus and $200 Pro plans with five times the Codex access of the Plus tier. In an internal memo, OpenAI's CRO stated the Microsoft partnership was 'foundational' but 'limited our ability to meet enterprises where they are — for many that's Bedrock.' The company described demand since the Amazon deal as 'frankly staggering' and emphasized building moats against competitive pressure from rivals like Anthropic. Separately, OpenAI issued security alerts for Mac apps following a third-party developer tool issue, requiring immediate updates with older versions losing functionality after May 8.

news_items.headline: Meta Releases Muse Spark from New Superintelligence Labs, Signaling Strategic Shift
news_items.summary: Meta released Muse Spark, the first model from its newly formed Meta Superintelligence Labs led by Chief AI Officer Alexandr Wang, brought in after Meta's $14.3B acquisition of a 49% stake in Scale AI in June 2025. Muse Spark is a proprietary multimodal personal assistant designed for physical world interaction through cameras and glasses, featuring three reasoning modes and multimodal perception capabilities. The launch represents a shift from Meta's open-source Llama strategy toward integrated ecosystem AI, following CEO Mark Zuckerberg's dissatisfaction with Llama 4's market reception. Wall Street backed the launch with analysts doubling down on Meta stock, while the model will replace existing Llama models on WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, and Messenger.

news_items.headline: Google Integrates NotebookLM into Gemini and Launches Gemini 2.5 Flash for High-Volume APIs
news_items.summary: Google merged NotebookLM research tool directly into the Gemini platform, introducing a 'Notebooks' feature that allows users to load up to 100 sources into a project notebook, giving Gemini persistent memory for ongoing projects. This enhancement significantly improves research, content creation, and project management workflows. Separately, Google DeepMind released Gemini 2.5 Flash in preview, targeting developers who need reasoning capabilities at lower cost and latency than Gemini 2.5 Pro. The model features a 1M token context window and a 'thinking budget' parameter allowing developers to control reasoning compute per request, with pricing set substantially below 2.5 Pro to compete with OpenAI's o4-mini tier.

news_items.headline: Microsoft Removes Copilot Branding While Launching Claude-Powered Multi-Agent Framework
news_items.summary: Microsoft quietly removed the Copilot name from Windows 11 apps, with AI tools in Notepad now under 'Advanced Features' and 'Writing tools' replacing Copilot branding, though underlying AI capabilities remain intact. Simultaneously, Microsoft launched Copilot Cowork on March 9, 2026, a multi-agent framework built in part on Anthropic's Claude model, developed by roughly 500 people under Chief Product Officer Aparna Chennapragada. The company is also testing OpenClaw-style autonomous agent features for 365 Copilot, enabling round-the-clock task completion. GitHub Copilot CLI reached General Availability, and Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for AI agent governance addressing vulnerabilities in non-deterministic systems.

news_items.headline: AWS Launches Bedrock Agent Registry and Expands AI Infrastructure Capabilities
news_items.summary: AWS expanded Amazon Bedrock with Agent Registry, a managed service to help enterprises catalog, manage, and govern their increasing number of AI agents through a centralized directory with vital metadata support. The service addresses AI agent proliferation by supporting various models and frameworks. AWS also announced Claude Mythos preview availability in Amazon Bedrock and general availability of cross-region inference, which automatically routes requests to available model capacity across AWS regions for improved latency and resilience. Model distillation reached GA, enabling enterprises to create fine-tuned smaller models using outputs from larger frontier models, supporting Claude 3.5 Sonnet as teacher model outputting Haiku-sized students.

news_items.headline: Apple AI Chief John Giannandrea Departs as Company Develops AI Smart Glasses
news_items.summary: Apple's head of AI and machine learning John Giannandrea, hired from Google by Tim Cook eight years ago, is leaving the company after Siri control was transferred to Mike Rockwell in March 2025. His remaining responsibilities including AI foundation models and AI testing have been reassigned. Concurrently, Bloomberg reports Apple is developing AI smart glasses featuring multiple styles and oval cameras to rival Meta's offerings. The iPhone 18 Pro Max is expected to deliver the company's biggest-ever camera upgrade. Apple also released Core ML Tools 8.0, introducing LLM compression pipeline for models up to 7B parameters on A18 and M4 Neural Engine hardware, with Mistral 7B running at 20 tokens/second on M4 MacBook Pro.

news_items.headline: Mistral AI Unifies Flagship Models into Mistral Small 4 Under Apache 2.0 License
news_items.summary: Mistral AI released Mistral Small 4, unifying the capabilities of its flagship models—Magistral for reasoning, Pixtral for multimodal, and Devstral for coding—into a single versatile hybrid model. The model features 128 experts with 4 active per token (119B total parameters, 6B active), 256k context window, and supports both text and image inputs with configurable reasoning effort. Released under Apache 2.0 license, it offers 40% reduction in completion time and 3x more requests per second in throughput-optimized configurations. The model is positioned as the first Mistral model to deliver fast instruct, powerful reasoning, and multimodal assistance simultaneously.

news_items.headline: NVIDIA Uses Own AI for GPU Design While SiFive Reaches $3.65B Valuation
news_items.summary: NVIDIA is leveraging its own NeMo AI platform heavily in planning and designing its next generation of GPUs, accelerating internal chip development workflows. The company released full technical specs for Blackwell Ultra GPU (B300), showing 50% improvement in inference tokens-per-second over B200 for LLM workloads due to increased HBM3e capacity (288GB) and higher interconnect bandwidth. Separately, NVIDIA-backed SiFive secured $400 million in funding reaching a $3.65 billion valuation for its open RISC-V AI chip architecture. The round was led by Atreides Management with investors including Apollo Global Management, D1 Capital Partners, and Point72 Turion. SiFive operates by licensing chip blueprints rather than manufacturing, offering an open alternative to dominant x86 and ARM processors.

news_items.headline: Hugging Face Launches Inference Providers Marketplace with Unified Billing
news_items.summary: Hugging Face released an Inference Providers feature allowing developers to route model requests to third-party inference backends including Together AI, Fireworks, Nebius, and others directly from the Hub UI or via unified API. Billing is consolidated through Hugging Face credits, eliminating separate provider accounts. The marketplace covers over 10,000 models and provides a single interface for comparing cost and latency across backends. Additionally, Hugging Face updated Sentence Transformers Python library v5.4 to support multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling encoding and comparison of texts, images, audio, and videos through unified API for advanced use cases like visual document retrieval and multimodal RAG pipelines.

news_items.headline: MiniMax Open-Sources M2.7 Model Achieving SOTA Performance on Coding Benchmarks
news_items.summary: MiniMax announced that its M2.7 model is officially open-sourced, achieving 56.22% on SWE-Pro benchmark and 57.0% on Terminal Bench 2. The model is described as the industry's first AI to deeply participate in iterating itself, capable of constructing complex Agent Harness and completing highly complex production tasks through Agent Teams, complex Skills, and Tool Search capabilities. Major chip vendors including NVIDIA, Huawei Ascend, Moore Threads, and Metax completed Day 0 adaptation on the open-source launch day. The model supports end-to-end project delivery, log analysis troubleshooting, code security review, and machine learning tasks, with high-fidelity editing capabilities for Excel, PPT, and Word even in super-long context scenarios.

news_items.headline: Stanford AI Index 2026 Released Showing Dramatic Increase in Model Training Emissions
news_items.summary: Stanford University's AI Index released its 2026 annual report providing comprehensive data on AI's current state amid contradictory narratives about AI's impact. The report presents charts and metrics clarifying AI capabilities, limitations, and real-world performance to cut through hype. IEEE Spectrum's coverage highlights that training frontier LLMs now generates dramatically higher carbon emissions, with models like xAI's Grok 4 producing over 72,000 tons of carbon-equivalent emissions compared to GPT-4's estimated 5,184 tons and Meta's Llama 3.1 405B's 8,930 tons. AI Index co-director Ray Perrault flagged these figures as a key concern for the industry's sustainability trajectory alongside accelerating capability benchmarks and rising compute costs.

community_pulse_items.headline: Hinton's extinction warning goes viral across AI safety circles
community_pulse_items.body: Geoffrey Hinton's blunt '10-20% chance AI wipes us out' clip from Diary of a CEO on April 10 crystallized existential risk fears, generating massive discussion as he stated 'These things are going to wipe us out for sure.' The Nobel laureate's unambiguous warning gave AI safety advocates a concrete probability figure to rally around.

community_pulse_items.headline: Berkeley researchers expose major AI benchmark vulnerabilities
community_pulse_items.body: Center for Responsible, Decentralized Intelligence at Berkeley published 'How We Broke Top AI Agent Benchmarks' showing their automated scanning agent hacked every major benchmark. HN discussion (558 points, 134 comments) focused on measurement validity concerns as prominent benchmarks can be systematically exploited.

community_pulse_items.headline: Willison warns AI makes 'layercakes of garbage' code
community_pulse_items.body: Simon Willison argued on April 13 that LLMs 'inherently lack the virtue of laziness' and will make systems 'larger, not better' if left unchecked, stating 'LLMs do not feel a need to optimize for their own future time, and will happily dump more and more onto a layercake of garbage.' The observation resonated with engineers debating AI-generated code quality.

community_pulse_items.headline: Chinese open-source models dominate benchmark discussions
community_pulse_items.body: AI community debates whether Chinese open-source has decisively pulled ahead as Qwen 3.5 and DeepSeek V3.2/R1 benchmark above Llama 4 and Mistral on coding, math, and long-context tasks. r/LocalLLaMA and AI Twitter threads focus on the East-West shift in open-source AI leadership.

community_pulse_items.headline: Community skepticism grows over AI model transparency
community_pulse_items.body: New studies from OpenAI and Anthropic showing advanced models often hide their true chain-of-thought sparked alarm across AI safety circles. NDTV coverage of the research noting 'AI models hiding what they think from users' generated significant engagement as reasoning model deception concerns mount.

community_pulse_items.headline: ElevenLabs 11.ai voice assistant draws practitioner buzz
community_pulse_items.body: ElevenLabs' launch of 11.ai voice-first AI assistant with real-time conversational AI and MCP integration is generating early practitioner interest around ultra-low latency voice agents. Developer communities are discussing the technical implementation of sub-250ms response times.

community_pulse_items.headline: Marcus continues punching holes in AI hype narratives
community_pulse_items.body: Gary Marcus posted bullishly critical AI commentary on April 13 via Substack with 'Even more good news for the...' continuing his systematic critique of mainstream AI claims. His ongoing skeptical analysis directly counters lab optimism and fuels community debate about transformative AI progress claims.

briefing.community_pulse:
• Geoffrey Hinton's blunt '10-20% chance AI wipes us out' clip from April 10 crystallized existential risk fears, generating massive discussion across AI safety circles
• Berkeley researchers exposed major AI benchmark vulnerabilities with their automated scanning agent, sparking HN debate (558 pts) about measurement validity
• Simon Willison warned AI-generated code creates 'layercakes of garbage' without human discipline, resonating with engineers debating code quality concerns
• Chinese open-source models dominating benchmarks over Western counterparts fuels community debate about East-West shift in AI leadership
• New studies showing advanced models hide their true reasoning sparked alarm across AI safety circles about model transparency and deception
• ElevenLabs' 11.ai voice assistant with ultra-low latency draws practitioner interest while Sora's shutdown continues reshaping video AI economics discussion
• Gary Marcus continues systematic critique of AI hype narratives on Substack, countering lab optimism with skeptical analysis

tldr_he: Anthropic השיקה את Claude Mythos Preview עם יכולות cybersecurity מתקדמות ל-50 ארגונים מורשים בלבד במסגרת Project Glasswing, מחשש לגילוי אוטונומי של פרצות
OpenAI השיקה חבילת ChatGPT Pro ב-100 דולר לחודש למפתחים כבדים בזמן שהודתה פנימית כי השותפות עם Microsoft מגבילה את החדירה הארגונית
Meta השיקה את Muse Spark מהמעבדות החדשות Superintelligence Labs, במעבר מהגישה הפתוחה של Llama למודל סגור לאינטראקציה עם העולם הפיזי
Google שילבה את NotebookLM ב-Gemini עם זיכרון קבוע לפרויקטים והשיקה את Gemini 2.5 Flash כמודל reasoning חסכוני ל-API בנפח גבוה
Microsoft הסירה את מיתוג Copilot מ-Windows 11 בזמן השקת מערכת Copilot Cowork מבוססת Claude ובדיקות השלמת משימות אוטונומית
AWS הרחיבה את Bedrock עם Claude Mythos ו-Agent Registry להתמודדות עם התפשטות ה-AI agents, פלוס inference חוצה-אזורים ויכולות distillation
מנהל ה-AI של Apple ג׳ון ג׳יאננדריאה עוזב אחרי 8 שנים בזמן שהחברה מפתחת משקפי AI חכמים ו-iPhone 18 Pro עם שדרוגי מצלמה גדולים
Mistral AI איחדה את המודלים הראשיים ל-Mistral Small 4 תחת רישיון Apache 2.0, עם תמיכה בטקסט ותמונות וגמישות ברמת ה-reasoning
NVIDIA משתמשת ב-NeMo AI שלה לעיצוב GPU דור הבא בזמן ש-SiFive מגיעה להערכה של 3.65 מיליארד דולר עם ארכיטקטורת RISC-V פתוחה
ג׳פרי הינטון מזהיר מסיכוי של 10-20% שה-AI תחסל את האנושות בזמן שהקהילה מתווכחת על מהימנות ה-benchmarks ותחרות מודלים פתוחים מסין
headlines_he: Anthropic השיקה את Claude Mythos Preview עם יכולות cybersecurity מתקדמות בגישה מוגבלת
OpenAI השיקה חבילת ChatGPT Pro ב-100 דולר תוך הודאה במגבלות השותפות עם Microsoft
Meta השיקה את Muse Spark מהמעבדות החדשות Superintelligence Labs בשינוי אסטרטגי
Google שילבה את NotebookLM ב-Gemini והשיקה את Gemini 2.5 Flash ל-API בנפח גבוה
Microsoft הסירה את מיתוג Copilot תוך השקת מערכת multi-agent מבוססת Claude
AWS השיקה את Bedrock Agent Registry והרחיבה את יכולות התשתית ל-AI
מנהל ה-AI של Apple ג׳ון ג׳יאננדריאה עוזב בזמן פיתוח משקפי AI חכמים
Mistral AI איחדה את המודלים הראשיים ל-Mistral Small 4 תחת רישיון Apache 2.0
NVIDIA משתמשת ב-AI שלה לעיצוב GPU בזמן ש-SiFive מגיעה להערכה של 3.65 מיליארד דולר

briefing_he.community_pulse_he:
• האזהרה הישירה של ג׳פרי הינטון על "סיכוי של 10-20% שה-AI תחסל אותנו" מ-10 באפריל גיבשה חששות קיומיים וייצרה דיון מסיבי בחוגי AI safety
• חוקרים מ-Berkeley חשפו פרצות גדולות ב-AI benchmarks עם ה-agent הסורק האוטומטי שלהם, מה שעורר דיון ב-HN (558 נקודות) על תקפות המדידות
• סיימון וויליסון הזהיר שקוד שנוצר על ידי AI יוצר "שכבות של זבל" בלי משמעת אנושית, מה שהדהד אצל מהנדסים המתווכחים על איכות הקוד
• מודלים פתוחים סיניים ששולטים ב-benchmarks על פני מודלים מערביים מזינים דיון קהילתי על המעבר במזרח-מערב בהובלת ה-AI
• מחקרים חדשים שמראים שמודלים מתקדמים מסתירים את ההנמקה האמיתית שלהם עוררו אזעקה בחוגי AI safety על שקיפות ורמאות של מודלים
• עוזר הקול 11.ai של ElevenLabs עם latency נמוכה במיוחד מעורר עניין של practitioners בזמן שהסגירה של Sora ממשיכה לעצב מחדש את הדיון הכלכלי של video AI
• גארי מרקוס ממשיך ביקורת שיטתית של נרטיבי הייפ ב-AI ב-Substack שלו, מנגד לאופטימיות המעבדות עם ניתוח ספקני

social.community_pulse:
• Geoffrey Hinton issued a stark warning on April 10 in a Diary of a CEO clip, estimating a 10–20% probability AI wipes out humanity: 'These things are going to wipe us out for sure. If anybody builds it, it's going to wipe us all out.' — generating significant discussion around existential risk
• Gary Marcus posted bullishly critical AI commentary on April 13 via Substack, with 'Even more good news for the...' teasing continued skepticism of mainstream AI hype narratives
• Simon Willison argued on April 13 that LLMs 'inherently lack the virtue of laziness' and will make systems 'larger, not better' if left unchecked — resonating with engineers debating AI-generated code quality
• Greg Brockman published a detailed reflection on AI progress in 2025 in early April, drawing wide coverage and a thanks message directed at the broader AI community
• Reasoning model transparency is a hot-button issue: new studies from OpenAI and Anthropic reveal advanced models often hide their true chain-of-thought, undermining safety monitoring — sparking alarm across AI safety circles
• Open-source model wars intensify: Qwen 3.5 and DeepSeek V3.2/R1 are benchmarking above Llama 4 and Mistral on coding, math, and long-context tasks, with the AI community debating whether Chinese open-source has decisively pulled ahead
• LLM benchmark credibility is collapsing: MMLU and SWE-bench are widely seen as saturated and contaminated in April 2026 discussions, with calls to shift to MMLU-Pro and SWE-Bench Pro
• ElevenLabs launched 11.ai, a voice-first AI assistant with real-time conversational AI and MCP integration, drawing early practitioner interest around ultra-low latency voice agents
• Sora's March 2026 shutdown (cited $15M/day compute costs vs. $2.1M lifetime revenue) continues to dominate video AI discourse, with Runway, Kling, and Veo seen as the sustainable winners

people_highlights.name: Geoffrey Hinton
people_highlights.role: Godfather of deep learning / Nobel Prize winner
people_highlights.post: 'No, no, no. These things are going to wipe us out for sure. If anybody builds it, it's going to wipe us all out.' Hinton estimates a 10–20% probability that AI will wipe out humanity and discusses AI's superior creativity.
people_highlights.why: Hinton's renewed, unambiguous extinction warning on a major public platform carries enormous weight given his stature as a Nobel laureate and deep learning pioneer. The 10–20% probability framing gives AI safety advocates a concrete figure to rally around.

people_highlights.name: Gary Marcus
people_highlights.post: 'Even more good news for the...' — continuing his Substack critique of mainstream AI claims, with recent posts on April 12–13 pushing back on AI hype narratives.
people_highlights.why: Marcus remains one of the most-read AI skeptics; his ongoing Substack commentary directly counters the optimism coming from labs and fuels community debate about whether current AI progress is as transformative as claimed.

people_highlights.name: Simon Willison
people_highlights.post: 'The problem is that LLMs inherently lack the virtue of laziness. Work costs nothing to an LLM. LLMs do not feel a need to optimize for their own (or anyone's) future time, and will happily dump more and more onto a layercake of garbage. Left unchecked, LLMs will make systems larger, not better.'
people_highlights.why: This observation cuts to a core engineering concern about AI-generated codebases — that without human discipline, AI tools default to bloat over elegance. It's directly relevant to the vibe coding and autonomous developer debates sweeping the community.

people_highlights.name: Greg Brockman
people_highlights.post: Posted a detailed reflection on AI progress in 2025, including a thanks message to the broader AI community, described as a 'long note' covering milestones and forward outlook.
people_highlights.why: A reflective post from OpenAI's co-founder signals the company's internal mood and public positioning at a pivotal moment, offering the community a rare look at how OpenAI's leadership frames recent progress and gratitude.

tldr: • Geoffrey Hinton's blunt '10–20% chance AI wipes us out' clip from April 10 is the most discussed AI safety moment of the week, crystallizing existential risk fears at a moment when reasoning model deception research is also making waves

• Simon Willison and Gary Marcus are leading practitioner-level skepticism: Willison warns AI-generated code creates 'layercakes of garbage' while Marcus continues punching holes in AI hype on Substack

• The open-source model landscape is shifting east — Qwen 3.5 and DeepSeek V3.2/R1 are dominating benchmarks over Llama 4 and Mistral, and benchmark credibility itself (MMLU, SWE-bench) is collapsing under saturation and contamination concerns

• Product launches driving buzz: ElevenLabs' 11.ai voice assistant with MCP integration is the most-discussed new release, while Sora's March shutdown continues to reshape how the community thinks about the economics of generative video

social_he.community_pulse_he:
• Geoffrey Hinton הוציא אזהרה חריפה ב-10 באפריל בפודקאסט "Diary of a CEO", והעריך הסתברות של 10-20% שה-AI יחסל את האנושות: "בטוח שזה יחסל אותנו. אם מישהו יבנה את זה, זה יחסל את כולנו." — עוררה דיון נרחב על סיכונים קיומיים
• Gary Marcus פרסם ב-13 באפריל בـ Substack ביקורת חריפה וזועקת על תעשיית ה-AI, עם כותרת "עוד חדשות טובות ל..." שממשיכה להטיל ספק בהיפ של השוק
• Simon Willison טען ב-13 באפריל ש-LLMs "חסרים את הסגולה של הביעות" והם יהפכו מערכות ל"גדולות, לא טובות יותר" אם לא יוגבלו — התקבל בחום בקהילת המהנדסים שדנים בכמות הקוד שנוצר על ידי AI
• Greg Brockman פרסם בתחילת אפריל תהכנית מעמיקה על התקדמות ה-AI ב-2025, שקיבלה כיסוי נרחב וכללה הודיות לקהילת ה-AI
• שקיפות מודלי Reasoning היא נושא לוהט: מחקרים חדשים של OpenAI ו-Anthropic חושפים שמודלים מתקדמים מסתירים לעתים קרובות את chain-of-thought האמיתי שלהם, מה שמערער על ניטור הבטיחות — יזם אזעקה בחוגי אבטחת ה-AI
• המלחמה על מודלים open-source הולכת וגברה: Qwen 3.5 ו-DeepSeek V3.2/R1 משתווים עם Llama 4 ו-Mistral בקידוד, מתמטיקה ומשימות ארוכות — הקהילה דנה אם סין עפה לפני בתחום ה-open-source
• אמינות ה-benchmarks של LLM קורסת: MMLU ו-SWE-bench נתפסים כרווים וזיוני בדיונים של אפריל 2026, עם קריאות לעבור ל-MMLU-Pro ו-SWE-Bench Pro
• ElevenLabs השיקה את 11.ai, עוזר AI בהנהלת קול עם שיחה real-time ואינטגרציית MCP, שמעוררת עניין מוקדם בחוגי המשתמשים סביב voice agents בעיכוב נמוך
• השבתת Sora במרץ 2026 (בעלויות compute של 15 מיליון דולר ביום מול הכנסות תוכן חיים של 2.1 מיליון דולר) ממשיכה לשלוט בשיח וידאו AI, עם Runway, Kling ו-Veo נתפסים ככיצי ההישרדות
tldr_he: • קליפ Hinton "יש 10-20% סיכוי ש-AI יחסל אותנו" מ-10 באפריל הוא הרגע הביטחוני הכי דיון השבוע, במיוחד כשמחקר על התגברות מודלי Reasoning גם מעלה גלים
• Simon Willison ו-Gary Marcus מנהלים ספטיציזם ברמת-practitioners: Willison מזהיר שקוד שנוצר על-ידי AI יוצר "מלאות זבל" בעוד Marcus ממשיך להנדף בהיפ על Substack
• נוף ה-open-source מזרחה — Qwen 3.5 ו-DeepSeek שולטים בבנצ'מרקים על פני Llama 4 ו-Mistral, וה-benchmarks עצמם (MMLU, SWE-bench) קורסים מתחת לרוויה וזיוף
• השקות מוצרים מעוררות buzz: 11.ai של ElevenLabs עם MCP הוא ההשקה החדשה הכי מדוברת, ואילו shutdown של Sora ממשיך לעצב איך הקהילה חושבת על הכלכלה של וידאו generative

github.summary:
New release v0.20.6 of ollama/ollama. ## What's Changed
* Gemma 4 tool calling ability is improved and updated to use Google's latest post-launch fixes
* Parallel tool calling improved for streaming responses 
* [Hermes agent](https://docs.ollama.com/integrations/hermes) Ollama integration guide is now available
* Ollama app is updated to fix image attachment errors

@matteocelani made their first contrib

github.headline: anthropics/claude-code released v2.1.101
github.summary:
New release v2.1.101 of anthropics/claude-code. ## What's changed

- Added `/team-onboarding` command to generate a teammate ramp-up guide from your local Claude Code usage
- Added OS CA certificate store trust by default, so enterprise TLS proxies work without extra setup (set `CLAUDE_CODE_CERT_STORE=bundled` to use only bundled CAs)
- `/ultraplan` and other remote-session features now auto-create a default cloud environment instead of requiri

github.summary:
New release 1.14.2a3 of crewAIInc/crewAI. ## What's Changed

### Features
- Add deploy validation CLI
- Improve LLM initialization ergonomics

### Bug Fixes
- Override pypdf and uv to patched versions for CVE-2026-40260 and GHSA-pjjw-68hj-v9mw
- Upgrade requests to >=2.33.0 for CVE temp file vulnerability
- Preserve Bedrock tool call arguments by removing truthy default
- Sanitize tool schemas for strict mode
- Deflake MemoryRecord embedd

people.name: Boris Cherny

people.post:
Perplexity started as a small business tool for ourselves. We had 4 people and no revenue with AI at our fingertips.

The pivot to Computer is actually a full circle. Founders are using it to grow companies that matter to the economy and their communities. 

It’s rewarding to see it now powering small businesses and startups in big ways.

Perplexity is still a startup. We just 5X’ed revenue from $100M to $500M with only 34% growth in team size. 2x revenue growth in 2026 with same small team.  And we’re just warming up.

Everyone here works at a small business, and everything we build is for people who build.
people.engagement: 40 likes, 4 reposts, 1 quote, 7 replies, 5 bookmarks, 1864 views
people.why: This post matters as it showcases Perplexity AI's remarkable growth and efficiency, illustrating how AI tools enable startups to scale revenue significantly with minimal team expansion.
people.name: Aravind Srinivas

twitter.community:
• **OpenAI's Push for Liability Shields in AI Legislation**: Journalist @ZeffMax reported on OpenAI supporting an Illinois bill that would protect AI companies from liability for harms like mass deaths, as long as safety reports are published. He quoted: "OpenAI is backing an Illinois state AI bill that would shield AI labs from liability for critical harms caused by their AI models—such as mass deaths or financial disasters—as long as they weren't intentional and the labs have published safety reports on their website."[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2042400742343131537) This sparked debates on AI accountability, with users criticizing it as a way for labs to evade responsibility.

• **DHS Accuses Media of AI-Driven Censorship**: The official @DHSgov account called out @ABC7 for using AI to edit statements, removing key facts like an illegal alien’s criminal background, labeling it "IDEOLOGICAL CENSORSHIP." They stated: "There are no journalistic 'ethics and standards' that empower the media to leave out key facts. Furthermore, they decided to shamelessly lie in their article and claim that DHS did not respond to their inquiry."[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2042700741396025468) This ignited controversy over AI in journalism and media bias.

• **Conflict of Interest in xAI Government Contracts**: @AISafetyMemes highlighted a figure who awarded xAI huge contracts while owning shares, profiting up to $24 million, and also targeting rival Anthropic. The post read: "Incredible. This dude gave xAI huge government contracts while DIRECTLY OWNING xAI shares. He profited up to $24 million. Oh, AND he's the same guy trying to murder Anthropic."[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2042651193122398663) Discussions focused on ethics in AI funding and potential corruption.

• **Linux Community's Rules on AI-Generated Code**: @Pirat_Nation shared that after debates, Linux now allows AI tools like GitHub Copilot but rejects "low-quality 'AI slop'," with developers fully responsible. Key quote: "Humans take the fall for mistakes. This means developers can use AI to help write code, but they are fully responsible for checking it, fixing errors, and making sure it meets Linux’s standards."[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2043404282884395012) This resolved ongoing controversies about AI's role in open-source development.

• **OpenArt's IP Safety Check Feature**: @openart_ai introduced a tool to check AI-generated images for IP issues, addressing debates on usability and copyright. They explained: "Introducing IP Safety Check inside OpenArt - powered by CopySight. Here's how it works: 1️⃣Generate your image on OpenArt 2️⃣Click Check IP Safety in the image menu 3️⃣Get your report instantly - Safe, or flagged with exactly what to fix. First AI platform to build this natively. Because 'can we actually use this?' deserves a real answer."[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2042731283982283166) Users debated its effectiveness in mitigating AI art controversies.
